title: Historical Browser Terminal Host Runtime Inventory
historical_note: This packet records one bounded Olares Phase 5 browser-terminal host-inventory fallback slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to `C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform` on 2026-05-07. It remains packet-history provenance, not a live execution instruction for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this packet JSON only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier `olares-phase-5-004b` browser-terminal host-inventory fallback slice
objective: Use authenticated Olares browser terminal access, if available, to capture the missing read-only host runtime inventory while workstation Remote-SSH remains blocked, and record that mesh-access recovery still remains a separate problem.
dependency_note: Packet 003 concluded that browser-terminal-assisted host inventory is the explicit fallback when operator interaction for local LarePass profile recovery is unavailable. This packet exists to recover host runtime truth without pretending that Remote-SSH or private-mesh access has been fixed.
required_inputs: The Packet 003 ruling that browser-terminal-assisted host inventory is the bounded fallback when operator interaction is unavailable, recorded in ops/agents/handoffs/2026-05-03-olares-phase-5-003-termpass-needslogin-blocker-audit-and-recovery-path-research-handoff.md
The Packet 001 partial result showing that runtime inventory is still unsatisfied because SSH was unavailable
Authenticated browser access to the Olares terminal surface
A pre-bounded command list for read-only host inventory only
constrained_outputs: A dated handoff recording whether authenticated browser-terminal access was available and sufficient for read-only host inventory
Read-only host runtime inventory for Docker, K3s or Helm, installed apps, ports, volumes, networks, and private-lane timers if terminal access succeeds
A statement that Remote-SSH and private-mesh recovery remain separate open blockers if browser-terminal inventory succeeds without fixing workstation mesh access
No installs, no promotions, no ingress changes, no auth changes, no service restarts, no AI-toolchain rollout, and no hosting changes
validation_steps: Open the authenticated Olares browser terminal only if it is already available through the current UI path; do not widen into auth-repair work inside this packet.
Run only pre-approved read-only host inventory commands covering Docker, K3s or Helm, installed apps, ports, volumes, networks, and private-lane timers.
Capture whether installed `forms-engine` and `p6-ingest` surfaces appear healthy or inspectable from the host side.
Capture whether private-lane timer surfaces and backup or restore-drill units are visible from the host side.
Record explicitly that mesh-access recovery and VS Code Remote-SSH viability remain separate unresolved questions unless independently revalidated during the packet.
Stop immediately if authenticated browser-terminal access is unavailable rather than widening into interactive auth repair, service mutation, or public-host SSH trust changes.
sql_permission_note: This packet authorizes authenticated browser-terminal read-only host inventory only. It does not authorize database writes, service mutation, auth repair, or any hosting or ingress changes.
closure_note: Not yet executed. This packet exists as the explicit fallback named by Packet 003 when operator-interactive local LarePass profile recovery is unavailable.
next_action: Execute this packet only when authenticated browser-terminal access is available and the priority is host runtime truth rather than workstation mesh recovery.
